Initial impressions of "Resisting the Virtual Life": dated in places & came before 9/11, Amazon, Google, social media etc.
but that forms part of its value as a time capsule of the anxieties and anticipations at the dawning of the "Information Superhighway".
but amazing how many of the essays are validated by events of the nearly 20 intervening years eg Amazon, Netflix, expanding corporate rights
upheaval in Egypt, financial crisis, lack of privacy oversight.
Amazing too how all the concerns seem to go in directions that fail to anticipate "user-generated content" and blogs
especially the model of Facebook where users simply by using consent to being vetted and profiled and targeted by vendors
and freely provide the pertinent info by "liking" things and friend finders and signing up for recommendations.
Much more to read but so far very recommended in any of this interests you, a Twitter follower.
